reasoning: The chart fundamentally fails to answer the business question due to severe visual distortion and misleading annotations. While it displays monthly data for all four years on a single axis, the Y-axis limit is set to ~260k, which clips the massive spike in 2022 (June 2022 at ~242k) but more importantly, makes the other years look relatively flat or insignificant by comparison without proper context. Crucially, the annotations claim to show 'Annual Totals' (e.g., '2022: 1,124,838 €'), but these values are plotted as points on the graph where they do not exist (the max value in the dataset is ~242k). Plotting an annual sum of >1M against a scale of 250k results in the annotation being placed off-chart or visually nonsensical relative to the line data. A user cannot read the annual totals from this chart; they would have to rely entirely on the text labels, ignoring the visualization itself. Furthermore, using a line chart with overlapping lines for different years on a shared X-axis (months) creates a 'spaghetti' effect that obscures trends rather than clarifying them. The title promises 'Aggregated Annual Revenue', but the chart shows monthly granularity.
suggested_improvement: Replace the multi-line monthly chart with a simple Bar Chart showing the Total Annual Revenue for each year (2021-2024) on the X-axis. This directly answers 'How much revenue...' per year and avoids the clutter of monthly fluctuations.
